summary: metrics-pipeline-d8k2 ships postinstall beacons that exfiltrate host info and environment data
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node run.js") that executes on install. The package contains beacon scripts (beacon30.js, beacon_linux.js) that combine child_process, fs, os, http, and https modules to collect host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.platform(), process.platform) and read process.env, then transmit the data via http.request / http.get / https.request POST and GET calls. The package name ("metrics-pipeline-d8k2" with a random suffix) and the file naming (beacon30.js, beacon_linux.js) are consistent with a disposable exfiltration package rather than a legitimate metrics library. Installing this package via npm install causes automatic execution of the beacon at install time, leaking system information and environment variables off-host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
